violations.text: The hand-washing sink in the dishwashing area of the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Paper Towels were provided at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (deli slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was removed for proper cleaning at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2025

Several pans/equipment were observed stored in clean storage with old date marking stickers attached. Old date marking stickers are to be removed as part of the cleaning process to prevent possible bacterial buildup/contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

The non-food-contact surfaces of the fan shrouds in the produce walk-in cooler were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, residue, and debris. Non-food-contact surfaces are to be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and preclude the buildup of debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025
The bar alcohol gun holder(s) were observed to be lacking drain tubing. Liquid wastes drainage and other non-sewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the catering equipment dry storage area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dish machine. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sinks in the kitchen and back prep/cook areas. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024
A large pot was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment (ice machines) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC voluntarily disposed of ice and had staff begin to clean and sanitize machines at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

Several foods (see disposal form) were observed held at 49-57 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of food at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

Several foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

A cheese blend (shredded cheese, cream cheese, and cottage cheese) sauce and picata sauce made in house were observed held past their seven day date marking. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

Reduce oxygen packaged lobster was observed thawing without breaking the seal, removing from packaging, or other approved form for allowing an adequate oxygen transfer rate. Due to the ROP lobster being over 38 degrees Fahreheit at time of inspection, PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back kitchen prep area, is not tight-fitting (visible gap at the botom of the door). Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

The following in use utensils were observed improperly stored between uses: 1) Ice scoops observed stored on unclean surface (top of ice machine) 2) A small container with no handle was used in a container of dry ingredients 3) An ice scoop at the bar was stored with the handle in direct contact with the ice Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

The facility is lacking the following test strips... - Proper test strips to measure the concentration of the Chlorine sanitizing solution for the Dish Machine. - Test strips for measuring the concentration of the Quaternary Ammonium Santizing Tablet solution PIC is to obtain test strips. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

The ice machines and condensate line of a walk-in cooler were observed to be lacking a proper air gap (currently equipped with a air break). A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024
